texto: There is abundant scholarship which establishes that the delegitimation of homosexual desire and the production of the naturally heterosexual, properly bi-gendered (unambiguously male or female) population of citizens, with the women respectably desexualized, is a process that is central to nation formation all over the globe.
explicacao: Citação de Nivedita Menon sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “There is abundant scholarship which establishes that the delegitimation of homosexual desire and …” articula reflexao, gender, heterosexuality em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e heterosexuality.

seo.titleSeo: "There is abundant scholarship which establishes that…" — Nivedita Menon | Metamensagem
seo.descriptionSeo: Citação de Nivedita Menon sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “There is abundant scholarship which establishes that the delegitimation of homosexual desire and …”…

frase_original: There is abundant scholarship which establishes that the delegitimation of homosexual desire and the production of the naturally heterosexual, properly bi-gendered (unambiguously male or female) population of citizens, with the women respectably desexualized, is a process that is central to nation formation all over the globe.

texto: [O]nce we give up on the idea that only heterosexuality is normal and that all human bodies are clearly either male or female, more and more kinds of bodies and desires will come into view. Perhaps also, one body may, in one lifetime, move through many identities and desires. The use of,queer’ then, is a deliberate political move, which underscores the fluidity (potential and actual) of sexual identity and sexual desire. The term suggests that all kinds of sexual desire and identifications are possible, and all these have socio-cultural and historical co-ordinates.
